free_text: First isomeric yield ratio measurements by direct ion
counting and implications for the angular momentum of
the primary fission fragments
- prelim.results
25 MeV proton beam from the K130
cyclotron at the University of Jyvaskyla.
IGISOL-3 and IGISOL-4 Ion Guide Isotope
Separator On-Line (IGISOL) facility and
JYFLTRAP double Penning trap mass spectrometer
using just the first (purification trap) of the two
traps and employing the sideband cooling technique.
The obtained spectra are Penning trap cyclotron
frequency spectra, inversely proportional to mass.
TOF depends on the mass and charge of the ions.
The isomeric yield ratio is defined as the high-spin
state yield over the total yield of the isotope.
The given uncertainties include the
statistical uncertainties and the uncertainties due
to the radioactive decay loss correction.
VS
SD: STATUS updated in Subents 002,006.
SD: Ref. EPJ/CS,146 added.
